markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Quick Vengeance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7882) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Guardian" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=859" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 49

**Prerequisites**
[Intercept Attack](/Actions.aspx?ID=3305); 

</column>

---

Indignant at any who would strike you or your allies, you get your revenge. When you use [Intercept Attack](/Actions.aspx?ID=3305) or [Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75), you are [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) on your next turn. You can use the extra action only to Stride closer to the enemy whose attack triggered that reaction or to Strike that enemy.
name: Quick Vengeance

[Shield from Spells](/Feats.aspx?ID=7883) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Guardian" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=859" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Great Shield Mastery](/Feats.aspx?ID=7884) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Guardian" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=859" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 49

</column>

---

You've learned how to maximize the defensive abilities of the largest shields. You continually benefit from [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=2307) without needing to spend an action, as long as you have a shield raised and it's a [tower shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=20), [fortress shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=7), or other shield that grants a higher circumstance bonus to AC when you Take Cover behind it. This benefit ends only if you cease having the shield raised, not from the normal conditions that end Take Cover.
name: Great Shield Mastery

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Commander Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7886) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
<trait label="Multiclass" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=115" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 52

**Archetype** [Commander](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=322)

**Prerequisites**
Intelligence +2

</column>

---

You gain the tactics class feature like a commander and gain your own folio; this folio contains two common mobility or offensive [tactics](Tactics.aspx) of your choosing. You can prepare one of these tactics whenever a commander would be able to prepare  tactics. You gain a commander's banner that grants you a 30-foot [aura](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=53) for the purposes of using your tactics, but the banner does not grant the commander's banner bonus to Will saves and DCs against fear effects. You become trained in commander class DC and Warfare [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41); if you were already trained in Warfare Lore, you become trained in another Lore skill of your choice.

<title level="2">Commander Dedication leads to...</title>
[Basic Field Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=7887), [Officer's Expertise](/Feats.aspx?ID=7890), [Officer's Mastery](/Feats.aspx?ID=7891), [Tactical Excellence](/Feats.aspx?ID=7888)
name: Commander Dedication

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 53 **Archetype** [Guardian](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=323) **Prerequisites** Strength +2; Constitution +2 --- You become trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36); if you were already trained in Athletics, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You become trained in guardian class DC.
You become trained in light armor and medium armor. If you already were trained in light armor and medium armor, you gain training in heavy armor as well. Whenever you gain a class feature that grants you expert or greater proficiency in any type of armor (but not unarmored defense), you also gain that proficiency in the armor types granted to you by this feat. If you have a class feature that grants you expert proficiency in unarmored defense and you're 13th level or higher, you also become an expert in the armor types granted to you by this feat. You can use the [Taunt](/Actions.aspx?ID=3304) action. Guardian Dedication leads to... [Basic Defender](/Feats.aspx?ID=7893), [Guardian Resiliency](/Feats.aspx?ID=7894), [Guardian's Intercept](/Feats.aspx?ID=7896), [Ironclad Fortitude](/Feats.aspx?ID=7898)
name: Guardian Dedication

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Aldori Duelist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7899) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 54

**Archetype** [Aldori Duelist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=324)

**Prerequisites**
trained in martial weapons

**Access**
You are from the Broken Lands region.

</column>

---

Your Aldori duelist training teaches you martial techniques and increases your dedication to the Aldori dueling sword. You  become trained in your choice of [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) or [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36); if you were already trained in that skill, you become an expert instead. You gain the [Additional Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=5114) feat for Dueling Lore; if you were already trained in Dueling Lore, you also become trained in a Lore skill of your choice. 

You have familiarity with [Aldori dueling swords](/Weapons.aspx?ID=535), treating them as martial weapons for the purposes of  proficiency. You gain access to Aldori dueling swords.

<title level="2">Aldori Duelist Dedication leads to...</title>
[Aldori Parry](/Feats.aspx?ID=7901), [Aldori Swordlord](/Feats.aspx?ID=7911), [Aldori's Retort](/Feats.aspx?ID=7907), [Devastating Duelist](/Feats.aspx?ID=7910), [Dueling Acumen](/Feats.aspx?ID=7900), [Duelist's Edge](/Feats.aspx?ID=7902), [Duelist's Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=7908), [One-on-One](/Feats.aspx?ID=7906), [Saving Slash](/Feats.aspx?ID=7905), [Swordlord Exile](/Feats.aspx?ID=7912), [Tempo Duelist](/Feats.aspx?ID=7909), [Unnerving Prowess](/Feats.aspx?ID=7904)
name: Aldori Duelist Dedication

    right="Feat 2"

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 54

**Archetype** [Aldori Duelist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=324)

**Prerequisites**
[Aldori Duelist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7899)

</column>

---

You're always ready for a new [duel](/Rules.aspx?ID=3084), and you've been trained to start and end fights quickly. When you're in a duel or can see exactly one creature, you can always roll Dueling [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) for initiative. If you do so while in a duel, you must choose from either Deception, Intimidation, or Perception when you roll for initiative; you're considered to have rolled the chosen skill for initiative for the purpose of dueling actions.
name: Dueling Acumen

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Duelist's Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=7908) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=152" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 55

**Archetype** [Aldori Duelist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=324)

**Prerequisites**
[Aldori Duelist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7899)

**Requirements**
You are wielding only an [Aldori dueling sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=535) and have your other hand or hands free; you are not [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73).

</column>

---

Many assume that a duelist's habit of fighting with one hand behind their back or on their hip is an insult or self-restriction. This could not be further from the truth. Fighting with a single hand dictates mastery and heightens the duelist's focus by narrowing points of interest, allowing them to move and react to changing situations with unnatural speed. While in this stance, you are [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89). You can use this extra action only to [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) or use a single-action activity gained from the Aldori duelist archetype. If you use your free hand for anything, this stance ends. When this stance ends, you become [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) until you rest for at least 10  minutes.
name: Duelist's Form
requirement_markdown: You are wielding only an [Aldori dueling sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=535) and have your other hand or hands free; you are not [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Aldori Swordlord](/Feats.aspx?ID=7911) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 55

**Archetype** [Aldori Duelist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=324)

**Prerequisites**
[Aldori Duelist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7899); you have taken the last name of Aldori, sworn to the Aldori swordpact, and demonstrated skill at swordplay by winning a duel using only a sword in the presence of an Aldori swordlord.

</column>

---

You are an Aldori swordlord. You can always obtain free room and board at the Aldori Academy for yourself and your allies.  You can use Dueling [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) instead of Diplomacy to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) or [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) provided that the target you are Making an Impression on would be aware of the Aldori swordlords or your Request is related to your standing as a swordlord. Whenever you successfully Strike a creature with an [Aldori dueling sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=535), you gain 5 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) (or 10 temporary Hit Point on a critical success) that last for 1 round.

**Special** You can’t take both this feat and [Swordlord Exile](/Feats.aspx?ID=7912).
name: Aldori Swordlord
prerequisite_markdown: [Aldori Duelist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7899); you have taken the last name of Aldori, sworn to the Aldori swordpact, and demonstrated skill at swordplay by winning a duel using only a sword in the presence of an Aldori swordlord.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Swordlord Exile](/Feats.aspx?ID=7912) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 55

**Archetype** [Aldori Duelist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=324)

**Prerequisites**
[Aldori Duelist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7899); you have broken or forsaken the Aldori swordpact.

</column>

---

You are a swordlord exile. You have abandoned or been abandoned by the Aldori Academy. Aldori swordlords are honor-bound to challenge you on sight. Additionally, your unbridled skill strikes terror in those around you. You can use Dueling [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) instead of Intimidation to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) and [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) while your [Aldori dueling sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=535) is visible to the target of your attempt. 

With broken pride, your blade is now a disgraced tool used to remove obstacles between you and your goals, whatever they may be. The first time you hit a creature with an Aldori dueling sword each round, you deal 5 additional precision damage.

**Special** You can’t take both this feat and [Aldori Swordlord](/Feats.aspx?ID=7913).
name: Swordlord Exile
prerequisite_markdown: [Aldori Duelist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7899); you have broken or forsaken the Aldori swordpact.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crossbow Infiltrator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7913) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 56

**Archetype** [Crossbow Infiltrator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=325)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48)

</column>

---

You have mastered stealthy weapons that allow you to strike carefully from a distance far enough to avoid reprisal, yet close enough to confirm your kills. You have familiarity with the [gauntlet bow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=538), [hand crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=427), and [repeating hand crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=539), treating the repeating  hand crossbow as a martial weapon for the purposes of proficiency and the gauntlet bow as a simple weapon for the purposes of proficiency. If you're at least an expert in any of these weapons, you gain access to that weapon's critical specialization effect. You also gain the Infiltrator's Draw action.  

**Infiltrator's Draw** <actions string="Single Action" /> **Frequency** once per round; **Effect**  You Interact to draw a gauntlet bow, hand crossbow, or repeating hand crossbow, then Strike with it. Alternatively, you can Strike with a loaded hand crossbow  or repeating hand crossbow you're already holding and then Interact to stow it.

<title level="2">Crossbow Infiltrator Dedication leads to...</title>
[Crescent Cross Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=7914), [Infiltration Assassination](/Feats.aspx?ID=7919), [Infiltrator's Blitz](/Feats.aspx?ID=7921), [Infiltrator's Reload](/Feats.aspx?ID=7916), [Lethargy Poisoner](/Feats.aspx?ID=7918), [Reloading Trick](/Feats.aspx?ID=7917), [Repeating Hand Crossbow Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=7915), [Unseen Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=7920)
name: Crossbow Infiltrator Dedication

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crescent Cross Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=7914) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 56

**Archetype** [Crossbow Infiltrator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=325)

**Prerequisites**
[Crossbow Infiltrator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7913)

</column>

---

You have familiarity with the [crescent cross](/Weapons.aspx?ID=345), an ingenious weapon that combines an arm-mounted, multi-chamber crossbow with a crescent-shaped blade. For the purposes of proficiency, you treat both its configurations  as simple weapons. 

Feats and abilities from this archetype that normally work with a gauntlet bow also work with your crescent cross, treating the melee form of the crescent cross as a gauntlet where appropriate. You gain the Crescent Spray action.  

**Crescent Spray** <actions string="Two Actions" /> (flourish) **Requirements** You are wielding  a crescent cross; **Effects** You Strike up to three times with the ranged version of your crescent cross. If it is  currently in its melee configuration, you can swap it to its ranged configuration as a free action before attempting these Strikes. You must have a bolt already chambered for each Strike and can Interact to swap to a different capacity chamber as a free action between each Strike. Each attack counts toward your [multiple attack penalty](/Rules.aspx?ID=2289), but you do not increase your penalty until you have made all your attacks.
name: Crescent Cross Training

<title

    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Repeating Hand Crossbow Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=7915) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 56

**Archetype** [Crossbow Infiltrator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=325)

**Prerequisites**
[Crossbow Infiltrator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7913)

</column>

---

The specific needs of your organization or mission warrant your use of the latest in ingenious crossbow technology. You gain access to [repeating hand crossbows](/Weapons.aspx?ID=539) and [repeater bandoliers](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3987). If you have [Running Reload](/Feats.aspx?ID=4875) or a similar feat that allows you to reload and perform another action or activity as a single action, you can use that action more efficiently with a repeating weapon. You can use the action as a 3-action activity to reload a magazine into a repeating weapon after you perform its other granted action, or as a 2-action activity if you use a magazine from a repeater bandolier.
name: Repeating Hand Crossbow Training

<traits>

</column>

---

You can fire off a single shot even when it seems like you are unprepared. You Interact to reload your hand crossbow or  gauntlet bow and attempt a ranged Strike with it.

**Special** If you have the [Repeating Hand Crossbow Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=7915) feat, you can use this feat with a repeating hand crossbow to load an entire magazine, but the speed means you can fire only one bolt before the magazine jams and becomes useless. You must fully replace a magazine as normal before firing with a repeating hand crossbow after using this ability.
name: Reloading Trick

    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lethargy Poisoner](/Feats.aspx?ID=7918) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 57

**Archetype** [Crossbow Infiltrator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=325)

**Prerequisites**
[Crossbow Infiltrator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7913); Trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)

</column>

---

You've learned more than the use of hand crossbows. During your daily preparations, you can prepare a dose of [lethargy poison](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3340) from ordinary materials in a wilderness or urban area. If you're an expert in Crafting, you can instead craft a dose of [stupor poison](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2016). You can  prepare two doses (of either poison) if you're a master in Crafting and three doses if you're legendary in Crafting. The save DC for this poison is equal to your class DC. Only you can use these poisons, and they expire the next time you make your daily preparations. The GM might decide that the area you're in is too barren to provide the materials you need for this feat.
name: Lethargy Poisoner

<traits>

</traits>

</column>

---

With a high-priority target in reach, you quickly move to eliminate them. Make a melee Strike using your gauntlet bow as a gauntlet. If the Strike is successful, you automatically latch onto the target, giving them the [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) condition, then make a ranged Strike against them with your gauntlet bow. This Strike does not trigger reactions normally triggered by ranged attacks. If you are holding or wearing an injury poison, you can apply it to the bolt used in the attack as a free action before making the ranged Strike.
name: Infiltration Assassination

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Infiltrator's Blitz](/Feats.aspx?ID=7921) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 57

**Prerequisites**
[Crossbow Infiltrator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7913)

</column>

---

Though your training is meant to ensure you don't get caught by your enemies, your missions are often high risk, and sometimes the only option you have left when things go wrong is to unleash a blistering torrent of violence while running away as fast as possible. 

You can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) any effect that is currently [immobilizing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) you. You gain a +10-foot circumstance bonus to your Speed and can Stride up to three times. At any point during each of these Strides, you can Strike with a weapon you have familiarity with from this archetype and can then immediately Interact to reload that weapon. 

After using Infiltrator's Blitz, you become fatigued for 1 minute.
name: Infiltrator's Blitz

markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 58 **Archetype** [Field Propagandist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=326) **Prerequisites** Charisma +2; Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38); Trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) --- Having devoted your life to crafting propaganda, you know that winning the hearts and minds of soldiers can be a deciding factor in securing the successful outcome of a war. You gain the Spread Propaganda exploration activity (see below). While you are not immune to propaganda, you are resistant to it. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Perception DC against attempts made by others to Lie to you. If you have the [Lie to Me](/Feats.aspx?ID=5177) skill feat, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Deception DC. You become trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47). If you were already trained, you become an expert instead. **Spread Propaganda** (exploration, secret) You spread propaganda about a faction (which may include an army, organization, or other particular group of people). This takes as long as it would normally take for you to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391) (typically 2 hours). At the end of this time, the GM rolls a secret Deception check to see how effective you were at spreading propaganda.
**Critical Success** Your propaganda spreads effectively, and for the next month, creatures that succeed on checks to Gather Information learn your propaganda over facts about the subject. It cannot be traced back to you. You also learn information as if you succeeded at a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) check about the faction.
**Success** As critical success, but the propaganda persists for 1 week and can be traced back to you by a character who critically succeeds at the skill check to Gather Information.
**Failure** Your propaganda fails to take hold.
**Critical Failure** Your propaganda is stunningly ineffective, and you take a –4 circumstance penalty to Deception checks to spread propaganda about the same subject for 1 week. In addition, the subject and its enemies become aware that you attempted to spread propaganda. Field Propagandist Dedication leads to... [A Tale to Believe In](/Feats.aspx?ID=7928), [Fabricate Truth](/Feats.aspx?ID=7923), [Invented Vulnerability](/Feats.aspx?ID=7925), [Invincible Army](/Feats.aspx?ID=7926), [Mass Delusion](/Feats.aspx?ID=7930), [Orator's Fillibuster](/Feats.aspx?ID=7927), [Predispose Settlement](/Feats.aspx?ID=7924), [The Truth as I See it](/Feats.aspx?ID=7929)
name: Field Propagandist Dedication

Choose a creature within 30 feet that you're aware of. Attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check against that target's Will DC. **Success** The target becomes stupefied 1 for 1 round.

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 58 **Archetype** [Field Propagandist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=326) **Prerequisites** [Field Propagandist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7922); Expert in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) --- You spend 7 days of downtime seeding propaganda through a settlement with a population of 2,500 or fewer. At 10th level, this increases to a settlement with a population of 10,000 or fewer, and at 16th level, you can affect settlements of any size. Select a faction or organization (such as the Pathfinder Society, the Hellknights, or the Firebrands) that this propaganda targets and whether you are improving or decreasing the settlement's attitude toward that faction. After this period, attempt a Deception or Diplomacy check against the hard DC of the level of the settlement. These changes last for 1 week before the people return to their original attitudes.
**Critical Success** You adjust the starting attitude of the settlement's inhabitants toward the targeted faction by two steps. In addition, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus on initiative rolls made in encounters that include members of your targeted faction while in the settlement for 1 week.
**Success** As critical success, but the inhabitants' attitude is adjusted by one step.
**Critical Failure** The propaganda attempt backfires. The attitude of the settlement's inhabitants toward you decreases by one step.
name: Predispose Settlement

You enumerate the many virtues and victories of your chosen allies, creating a story of their invincibility so convincing that  it is as good as the truth. Choose an ally you can see and attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) or [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check against the hard DC for the target's level. On a success, the target gains [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to either bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage, chosen when you use this ability; the type of damage resisted is usually tied to the story you tell about the character, such as giving them piercing resistance after telling a story about  the time they rushed through a rain of arrows to take on an enemy encampment. 

You can instead attempt your check against a very hard DC for the target's level to give them resistance to two damage types, adding acid, fire, cold, electricity, and sonic to the damage types you can grant resistance to.
name: Invincible Army

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 58 **Archetype** [Field Propagandist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=326) **Prerequisites** [Field Propagandist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7922) --- When you seek to convince others of the worth of your cause, you are capable of unleashing such an entrancing torrent of rhetoric and metaphor that it is all but impossible to look away from you. When you begin a conversation or similar attempt with the goal of [Making an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392), all creatures who are observing you become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) unless they succeed at a Will save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. Creatures who are familiar with you and your verbal techniques, such as allies you've known for at least several days, are immune to this effect, though newer allies who have not heard one of your speeches before might still be affected.

Affected creatures remain fascinated for as long as you continue your filibuster, though they can attempt a new save to end the effect at the end of each minute while your filibuster lasts. A creature who succeeds at a saving throw to end the fascinated condition from your filibuster is also unaffected by your attempt to Make an Impression.

You can continue your filibuster indefinitely, but after every 10 minutes you must attempt a Fortitude saving throw against a hard DC for your level. Failing this save results in your filibuster ending and prevents you from attempting another filibuster for 1 hour.
name: Orator's Fillibuster

feat_markdown: The Truth as I See it
markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 58 **Archetype** [Field Propagandist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=326) **Prerequisites** [Field Propagandist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7922) --- Your knack for spinning a believable reality from threads of story is so practiced that sometimes even you begin to believe what you're saying. When you use [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) to Lie, you can carefully structure your deceits so that each individual statement you make is the truth, from a certain perspective. Once per day when using Deception to Lie, you can roll twice and take the better result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect.

You don't take a penalty to Deception checks while subject to the [_ring of truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656) spell or similar effects. Whenever you are subject to _ring of truth_ or a similar effect, you can attempt a Deception check when you first begin speaking to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) the spell's effects, with a counteract rank equal to half your level (rounded up); succeeding at this check doesn't end the spell or effect, but it does cause it to indicate that you are speaking the truth, even when you are actually lying.
name: The Truth as I See it

markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 60 **Archetype** [Guerrilla](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=327) **Prerequisites** Trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48); Trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) --- You're skilled in subtle tactics and using the environment against your enemies. You become trained in your choice of [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) or [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50); if you are already trained in both, you become an expert in one instead. When you take this dedication, choose a single, discrete urban or wilderness location, such as “Mzali” or “the Verduran Forest”; this becomes your favored location. When you are in this location and [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) by all enemies, you can [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) without attempting a Stealth check as long as you move no more than half your Speed. During exploration, this also allows you to automatically approach within 15 feet of other creatures while Avoiding their Notice, as long as they aren't actively [Searching](/Actions.aspx?ID=2630) or on guard.

You can change your favored location by spending 1 week of downtime in the new location you would like to set as your favored location. Guerrilla Dedication leads to... [Battlefront Sabotage](/Feats.aspx?ID=7938), [Deathblow](/Feats.aspx?ID=7941), [Frightful Attrition](/Feats.aspx?ID=7939), [Guerrilla Weaponry](/Feats.aspx?ID=7932), [Hit and Run](/Feats.aspx?ID=7934), [Lonely Army](/Feats.aspx?ID=7940), [Rally Support](/Feats.aspx?ID=7936), [Snare Expert](/Feats.aspx?ID=7933), [Terrain Ghost](/Feats.aspx?ID=7942)
name: Guerrilla Dedication

<title

    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Guerrilla Weaponry](/Feats.aspx?ID=7932) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 60

**Archetype** [Guerrilla](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=327)

**Prerequisites**
[Guerrilla Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7931)

</column>

---

You are especially skilled in transforming simple weapons that can be easily assembled from readily available materials into deadly weapons. In your hands, a blowgun's base damage increases from 1 to a 1d4 damage die, and it gains the deadly  d4 trait. In addition, you gain the following action.  

**Guerrilla Assault** <actions string="Single Action" /> **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** You Interact to reload a sling or blowgun and then Strike. If the Strike is successful and you were undetected or unnoticed by the target when you made the attack, you are now [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) from the target after the attack, as they cannot tell  where the attack came from.

<title level="2">Guerrilla Weaponry leads to...</title>
[Poisoned Sticks and Stones](/Feats.aspx?ID=7935)
name: Guerrilla Weaponry

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Poisoned Sticks and Stones](/Feats.aspx?ID=7935) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 60

**Archetype** [Guerrilla](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=327)

**Prerequisites**
[Guerrilla Weaponry](/Feats.aspx?ID=7932)

</column>

---

You prepare your sling stones with small, edged grooves to enable them to deliver poison, and you have learned other  techniques for poisoning your weapons. You can apply injury poisons to sling bullets, allowing them to deliver such poisons  on a successful Strike. 

During your daily preparations, you can prepare a number of simple injury poisons equal to your level that can only be applied to sling bullets or blowgun darts. These follow the rules for [injury](/Traits.aspx?ID=635) poisons, except that they deal 1d4 poison damage with no saving throw. Only you can apply these poisons properly, and they expire at your next daily preparations. 

**Special** If you later gain the [Poison Weapon](/Feats.aspx?ID=4933) feat (such as from the poisoner archetype or the rogue multiclass archetype), you can apply your injury poisons to any weapon that is normally a valid receptacle for injury poisons, but you do not gain additional simple injury poisons during your daily preparations.

<title level="2">Poisoned Sticks and Stones leads to...</title>
[Deathblow](/Feats.aspx?ID=7941), [Venomous Weapons](/Feats.aspx?ID=7937)
name: Poisoned Sticks and Stones

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 61 **Archetype** [Guerrilla](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=327) **Prerequisites** [Guerrilla Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7931) **Requirements** You have a free hand. --- You know methods of sabotaging your enemy's war machines and weaponry. You can attempt to sabotage a siege weapon or vehicle with a [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) check against the standard DC of the weapon or vehicle's level.
**Critical Success** The next time the weapon is Launched or the vehicle moves, it targets or moves to a square of your choice instead of its intended target.
**Success** As critical success, but the weapon or vehicle targets or moves to a random square.
**Failure** The sabotage fails. Additionally, you can use Battlefront Sabotage to sabotage a weapon an enemy is wielding. Choose a creature wielding a melee or ranged weapon within your reach and attempt a Thievery check against their Reflex DC. On a success, the weapon's wielder must succeed at a DC 11 flat check the next time it attempts a Strike with that weapon or the action is disrupted, as part of the weapon's structural integrity falters. The creature is then temporarily immune to Battlefront Sabotage of their weapons for 10 minutes.
name: Battlefront Sabotage
requirement_markdown: You have a free hand.

A creature that didn't see you directly (such as if you're invisible or remained hidden after the attack) takes a –2 circumstance penalty to this save. Regardless of the result, each creature is temporarily immune to your Frightful Attrition for 10 minutes.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 3 and fleeing for 1 round.

>
[Lonely Army](/Feats.aspx?ID=7940) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 61

**Archetype** [Guerrilla](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=327)

**Prerequisites**
[Guerrilla Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7931)

**Requirements**
You are in your favored location; you are currently [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), unobserved, or unnoticed by all enemies; and you are not [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73).

</column>

---

You move and attack with deadly silence, unbelievable speed, and unlimited ferocity. Strike with a [blowgun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=424) or [sling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=430); if the Strike is successful, the enemy cannot tell where it came from and you remain hidden. Immediately after the successful Strike, you can [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) to a new location, Interact to reload a blowgun or sling, and Strike again, remaining hidden on a successful Strike. If the second Strike is successful, you can Sneak, Interact to reload, and then Strike with a sling or blowgun a third time, remaining hidden after a  successful Strike.  

After using Lonely Army, you are fatigued for 1 minute.
name: Lonely Army
requirement_markdown: You are in your favored location; you are currently [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), unobserved, or unnoticed by all enemies; and you are not [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73).

**Prerequisites**
[Guerrilla Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7931); [Poisoned Sticks and Stones](/Feats.aspx?ID=7935)

</column>

---

Your carefully prepared and placed shot brings instant death to your target. Attempt a Strike with a [blowgun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=424) or [sling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=430) using ammunition that you have poisoned with your simple injury poison. If the Strike is successful and you are hidden from the target, or unnoticed or undetected by them, the target must succeed at a Fortitude save against your class DC or die; this is a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) and [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) effect. A creature that survives is temporarily immune to the instant death effect of Deathblow for 1 day.
name: Deathblow

markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 62 **Archetype** [Iridian Choirmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=328) **Prerequisites** Trained in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) --- When you take this dedication, choose one willing, non-minion ally to be your student in the ways of the Iridian Choir. As part of your training in this archetype, you teach your student the necessary mindset and techniques; they gain the benefits of your abilities without having to take any feats of their own. You gain the benefits of your Iridian Choirmaster archetype feats only if both you and your student are alive and conscious.
You and your student can trade nonverbal messages as a single action, which have the effects of the [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598) spell but are non-magical. This requires a single action, has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), and [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642) traits, and requires that you be able to see each other. If your student dies, or if you and your student choose to part ways, you can choose and develop a new student by spending 1 week of downtime training another willing, non minion ally. Iridian Choirmaster Dedication leads to... [Ensnaring Disarm](/Feats.aspx?ID=7946), [Lesson of Bonded Eyes](/Feats.aspx?ID=7947), [Lesson of Mutual Gambits](/Feats.aspx?ID=7949), [Lesson of Sympathetic Vulnerability](/Feats.aspx?ID=7950), [Lesson of the Broken Wing](/Feats.aspx?ID=7944), [Lesson of the Circling Gale](/Feats.aspx?ID=7945), [Lesson of the Splintered Aegis](/Feats.aspx?ID=7951), [Lesson of Unified Elusion](/Feats.aspx?ID=7953), [Pupil's Guard](/Feats.aspx?ID=7954), [Repeat Lesson](/Feats.aspx?ID=7948), [Seize Advantage](/Feats.aspx?ID=7952), [Shared Mind, Shared Heart](/Feats.aspx?ID=7957), [Simultaneous Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=7956)
name: Iridian Choirmaster Dedication

This Strike doesn't count toward your student's multiple attack penalty, and their multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike.

</title>

<traits>

---

Your thoughts and dreams are your student's thoughts and dreams, and vice versa. You develop a permanent mental  connection, allowing you to communicate telepathically as long as you're on the same plane. When one of you fails a save against a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect, the other can choose to take the results of the failed save instead. If both of you are targeted by the same mental effect requiring a saving throw, you and your student take the better of each of your die rolls for the save. You each still add your own save modifier to the die result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect.
name: Shared Mind, Shared Heart

markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 66 **Archetype** [Necrologist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=330) **Prerequisites** ability to cast spells from spell slots, ability to cast [_summon undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1706) --- Your studies of the lists of the dead allow you to call forth a horde of undead with a brief incantation. You can summon your horde with the Raise the Horde activity, and you can use the Mobbing Assault action while your horde is raised to command it to attack. The eerie connection you have with your horde precludes you from also having an animal companion or any other companion (such as a follower), though if an ability allows you to have more than one follower (such as with the [Additional Follower](/Feats.aspx?ID=7995) feat of the [captain](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=332) archetype), you can count your horde as one. You have enough control over your horde that it doesn't attack you or your allies.
When raised, your horde is Huge and has a speed of 20 feet. It has the [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) traits. It can be attacked. Though it's made of several undead creatures, it can't share the same space as other creatures (unless you have certain feats). Due to your eldritch connection with your horde, it uses your AC, saves, and other defensive statistics like skill DCs, but is immune to the [grappled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), and [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) conditions. The horde has [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) equal to your level to physical damage, but [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) equal to your level to area and splash damage. You share an animating force, so any damage that would be dealt to the horde is dealt to you instead, though you take damage only once from any ability that includes both you and the horde in the area of effect (though you take the greater amount of damage). **Raise the Horde** (concentrate, magical, manipulate) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** Your undead horde appears in an unoccupied space within 30 feet. Choose whether your horde consists of skeletons or zombies, which affects the type of damage it deals with Mobbing Assault. Your horde remains summoned until the end of your next turn, but you can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the effect for up to 1 minute. The effect also ends if you end your turn more than 120 feet away from the horde. When you first use Raise the Horde and each time you Sustain the effect, you can have the horde Stride. When Raise the Horde ends, the undead that comprise your horde collapse lifelessly to the ground and quickly rot away. They can no longer be targeted or used for actions. **Mobbing Assault** (concentrate) **Requirements** Your horde has been raised; **Effect** Each enemy in a 5-foot emanation around your horde takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage (if your horde is zombies) or 2d6 slashing damage (if your horde is skeletons) with a basic Reflex save against your spell DC. At 10th level and every 4 levels thereafter, the damage increases by 2d6. Necrologist Dedication leads to... [Danse Macabre](/Feats.aspx?ID=7971), [Death Rattle](/Feats.aspx?ID=7974), [Deathguard](/Feats.aspx?ID=7968), [Dismal Harvest](/Feats.aspx?ID=7965), [Ghostsong](/Feats.aspx?ID=7969), [Grasping Corpses](/Feats.aspx?ID=7966), [Preserve the Horde](/Feats.aspx?ID=7970), [Shambling March](/Feats.aspx?ID=7967), [Shift Horde](/Feats.aspx?ID=7972)
name: Necrologist Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: ability to cast spells from spell slots, ability to cast [_summon undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1706)

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=73" />

**Frequency**
once per 10 minutes

**Prerequisites**
[Necrologist Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7964)

**Requirements**
Your horde is raised.

</column>

---

Your horde can drag unwilling victims along with it as it shambles forward. Your horde Strides up to its Speed and can move through the spaces of Large and smaller creatures, but it must end its movement in an unoccupied space. Each creature whose space it moves through is subjected to the horde's Mobbing Assault, attempting a basic Reflex save as usual. A creature who fails the saving throw is also [Repositioned](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379) to an unoccupied square of your choice adjacent to the horde's final position. You can't move the creature into or through obstacles.
name: Danse Macabre
requirement_markdown: Your horde is raised.

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 67 **Archetype** [Necrologist](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=330) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes **Prerequisites** [Ghostsong](/Feats.aspx?ID=7969) **Requirements** Your horde is raised and consists of spirits. --- The voices of the spirits you command spark fear in the hearts of your enemies. You Sustain your horde and command those spirits to shriek in unison. Each living enemy within a 20 foot emanation from your horde takes 5d10 mental damage, depending on its Will saving throw against your spell DC. This damage increases by 1d10 at 14th level and every 2 levels thereafter.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is frightened 3.
name: Wailing Dead
requirement_markdown: Your horde is raised and consists of spirits.

<traits>

---

When your skeletons and zombies attack, their skeletal claws and rotting hands clutch and tear flesh. Weaker foes are caught in their grip. You command your horde to perform a Mobbing Assault. Instead of being [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), a creature who takes physical damage from failing the saving throw is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) by your horde ([restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) on a critical failure). The DC to Escape from your horde is equal to your spell DC. Creatures grabbed by your horde that fail a saving throw against the horde's Mobbing Assault take 2d6 persistent bleed damage.
name: In the Horde's Grip

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[War Mage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7976) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Class" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=397" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 68

**Archetype** [War Mage](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=331)

**Prerequisites**
You have a spellcasting class feature.

</column>

---

Your studies into the battlefield applications of magic have made your spells particularly effective at disrupting enemy  formations or manipulating enemy troops into positions where they are more vulnerable to wide-scale magical attacks. When  you cast a non-cantrip spell that deals damage in an area, choose a number of targets equal to your Intelligence modifier who failed their saving throw against the effect. Move each one up to 10 feet from their original position after they take damage and any other effects from the spell. You can't move a creature into or through obstacles. A Medium or smaller creature counts as one target; a Large creature counts as two Medium creatures; and a Huge creature counts as four Medium  creatures. You can't move Gargantuan creatures in this way.  

You also gain the [Additional Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=5114) general feat for Warfare [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41). If you were already trained in Warfare Lore, you become trained in a Lore skill of your choice.

<title level="2">War Mage Dedication leads to...</title>
[Arcana of Iron](/Feats.aspx?ID=7980), [Intimidating Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7981), [Mage's Field Dressing](/Feats.aspx?ID=7977), [Shield Spell Reinforcement](/Feats.aspx?ID=7978), [Shielding Formation](/Feats.aspx?ID=7982), [Siege Ritualist](/Feats.aspx?ID=7979), [Spellshield](/Feats.aspx?ID=7983)
name: War Mage Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: You have a spellcasting class feature.

</column>

---

As your spell takes hold on your ally, you use some of its magic to quickly dress their wounds. You use Battle Medicine on one ally affected by the required spell. You do not need to be holding or wearing a [healer's toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2727), as you conjure glowing threads, bandages, or similar supplies out of pure magic, which adds the arcane trait to Battle Medicine.

    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shield Spell Reinforcement](/Feats.aspx?ID=7978) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Spellshape" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=513" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 68

**Archetype** [War Mage](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=331)

**Prerequisites**
[War Mage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7976); you can cast the [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671) cantrip

**Requirements**
You are wielding a shield.

</column>

---

You can empower your defensive magic by channeling it through a physical shield. If your next action is to cast the _shield_ cantrip, you use your shield as a locus to cast the spell, raising a magical barrier in the form of a large force projection of your worn shield. You can choose for an adjacent ally to gain the benefits of the _shield_ spell instead of yourself. If the ally would take damage from a physical attack while protected by your shield cantrip, you can use your reaction to [Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75) with the spell on their behalf.
name: Shield Spell Reinforcement
prerequisite_markdown: [War Mage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7976); you can cast the [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671) cantrip

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 69

**Archetype** [War Mage](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=331)

**Prerequisites**
[War Mage Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7976)

</column>

---

Your shield becomes a bonded item capable of storing your spells. You gain the arcane bond class feature and the [Drain Bonded Item](/Actions.aspx?ID=2260) action. You must select a shield as your bonded item. When you make your daily preparations, you can prepare one fewer wizard spell to infuse that magic into your bonded item. This spell must be at least 1 rank lower than the highest-rank wizard spell slot you have. When you Drain your Bonded Item to [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=3271), that spell is automatically heightened to the rank of spell you infused into your shield, no matter what rank you originally prepared it at.
name: Spellshield

As your prowess with weapons increases, so does the ferocity of magic you channel through those weapons. When you use  [Bespell Strikes](/Feats.aspx?ID=5028), you gain access to the critical specialization effect of the chosen weapon or unarmed attacks used with Bespell Strikes. If you critically hit with an attack modified by Bespell Strikes, you deal an additional 1d8 damage of the same type as the extra damage from Bespell Strikes; this damage is added after all other calculations and is not multiplied as part of the critical hit.

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cushion Landing](/Feats.aspx?ID=7986) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 70

**Prerequisites**
[Cavalier Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6286); Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)

**Trigger**
You fall from a height of 15 feet or higher.

**Requirements**
Your mount can reach your landing location with a single Stride.

</column>

---

As you fall, your mount dashes to your rescue. Your mount Strides up to its Speed, positioning itself below you. Rather than falling on your mount, you [Mount](/Actions.aspx?ID=2314) your mount. Treat the triggering fall as if it were 15 feet shorter, and you don't land prone (though both you and your mount both take damage from the fall based on what you would normally take for any remaining distance).
name: Cushion Landing
requirement_markdown: Your mount can reach your landing location with a single Stride.

</title>

---

You [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) to order your mount to Stride (or to Burrow, Climb, Fly, or Swim if it has the corresponding movement type). At any point during this movement, it must move adjacent to you. As it passes you, you [Mount](/Actions.aspx?ID=2314) your mount. You can increase the number of actions you use with Dashing Pickup to 2 to make your mount Stride twice, or to 3 to make your mount Stride four times; you must use enough actions to ensure your mount is able to move adjacent to you at some point during this movement, and you cannot use this activity if it is not possible for your mount to do so.

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Acclimated Mount](/Feats.aspx?ID=7991) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Champion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=24" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 71

**Prerequisites**
[Faithful Steed](/Feats.aspx?ID=5885); Trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49)

</column>

---

By the grace of your god, your mount can overcome the natural challenges and hazards present in a specific terrain. Choose  aquatic, arctic, desert, forest, mountain, plains, sky, swamp, or underground. Your mount must have a swim Speed to select  aquatic, and a fly Speed to select sky. Your mount ignores the effects of [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) while in the selected terrain.  

If you become an expert in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49), you can pray during your daily preparations each day to change the type of terrain to which your mount is acclimated.
name: Acclimated Mount

If your mount ends its movement on a surface that can't support it, your mount (and you) fall in or it collapses as normal.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Captain Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7994) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 82

**Archetype** [Captain](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=332)

**Prerequisites**
Charisma +2; you don't have an [animal companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=2113), [construct companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=1600), or other companion that functions similarly

</column>

---

You become trained in your choice of [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40); if you are already trained in both of these skills, you become trained in a skill of your choice. You also gain your choice of the [Group Impression](/Feats.aspx?ID=5155) or the [Group Coercion](/Feats.aspx?ID=5154) skill feat. If you already have both of these skill feats, you gain another 1st level skill feat for which you qualify.  

You also gain the assistance of a dedicated follower, who has agreed to accompany you on your journeys. They have the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) trait and begin as a novice follower. [See followers](Followers.aspx) for more information.

**Special** Once you have a follower, you can never take a feat or class feature that grants an animal companion or another companion that prevents you from having an animal companion.

<title level="2">Captain Dedication leads to...</title>
[Additional Follower](/Feats.aspx?ID=7995), [Cadre](/Feats.aspx?ID=7998), [Coordinated Tactics](/Feats.aspx?ID=8001), [Experienced Follower](/Feats.aspx?ID=7996), [Leader of All](/Feats.aspx?ID=7997), [Legendary Leader](/Feats.aspx?ID=8003), [Peerless Captain](/Feats.aspx?ID=8004), [Tactical Guidance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7999)
name: Captain Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Charisma +2; you don't have an [animal companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=2113), [construct companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=1600), or other companion that functions similarly

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Additional Follower](/Feats.aspx?ID=7995) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 82

**Archetype** [Captain](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=332)

**Prerequisites**
[Captain Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7994)

</column>

---

Another follower joins you in your travels. They are a [novice follower](Followers.aspx) that has the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) trait. 

**Special** You can select this feat more than once, gaining an additional follower each time, to a maximum of four total followers.

<title level="3">Multiple Followers</title> The captain archetype gives you the opportunity to have multiple followers at one time—up to four followers— but only one of those followers, your “active follower,” participates in combat or exploration activities; the rest hang back, ready to be called upon if needed. As soon as you gain a second follower from the captain archetype, you also gain Call Follower to switch your active follower.

<document level="2" id="action-3454" />
name: Additional Follower

    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Experienced Follower](/Feats.aspx?ID=7996) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Archetype** [Captain](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=332)

**Prerequisites**
[Captain Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7994)

</column>

---

Your followers' skills have developed over the course of your  adventures. All your followers become [experienced followers.](Followers.aspx)  

Additionally, your followers have grown accustomed to the perils of battle, developing the confidence to attack foes  without your direction. During an encounter, even if you don't use the [Direct Follower](/Actions.aspx?ID=3453) action, your active follower spend can still use 1 action that round on your turn to Stride or Strike. They can do this at any point during your turn, as long as you aren't currently taking an action. If they do, that's all the actions they get that round—you can't Direct them later.

<title level="2">Experienced Follower leads to...</title>
[Veteran Follower](/Feats.aspx?ID=8000)

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 82

**Archetype** [Captain](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=332)

**Prerequisites**
[Captain Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7994)

</column>

---

You accept anyone who would follow you on your adventures. You can take followers from any common ancestry, and from uncommon or rare ancestries at the GM's discretion. Your follower gains the traits, size, Speeds, languages, and other special abilities listed in their ancestry. For example, elf followers have the elf and humanoid traits, a 30-foot  speed, low-light vision, and the typical languages for elves. Followers don't use their ancestry's Hit Points or attribute boosts and flaws. They also don't gain abilities that would allow them to use actions beyond the standard ones available to a follower, such as [Activating an Item](/Actions.aspx?ID=100) or [Casting a Spell.](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734)

<title level="3">Follower Ancestries</title> Since humans are the most populous ancestry in Golarion and don’t have any special abilities, they are presented as the default ancestry for followers. The GM can consider giving captains the Leader of All feat for free if humans aren’t the most common ancestry in their campaign setting, or if they aren’t concerned with players choosing to avoid human followers in favor of more mechanically powerful options.
name: Leader of All

markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 83 **Archetype** [Captain](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=332) **Prerequisites** [Captain Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7994); expert in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) --- Between your daring adventures, people seek you out. A loyal cadre of retainers joins you. These individuals don't function as followers and won't accompany you into dangerous areas or situations. However, they can train by your side, assist you in creating items, or help you spread your heroic tales.
When you are in a place where your cadre could reasonably assist you, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to all skill checks to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385), [Create a Forgery](/Actions.aspx?ID=2402), [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=2364), and [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368), as well as other downtime activities at the GM's discretion. Your cadre also helps you train, making [retraining](/Actions.aspx?ID=2634) faster than normal. For every 5 days you spend retraining, you make a week's worth of progress.
name: Cadre

---

Your fame has spread throughout the lands. When you first attempt to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) or [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on an intelligent creature, the GM rolls a secret DC 11 flat check to see if they have heard of you. On a success, you gain a +2 circumstance modifier to the skill check for that action. The GM can adjust this flat check as appropriate for circumstances in which you may be more or less known. 

You can also leverage your notoriety and your network of assistants and admirers to earn money. You can [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=2364) using the skill you used to meet the prerequisites of this feat. When you do, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the  check; this bonus increases to a +3 if you have the [Cadre](/Feats.aspx?ID=7998) feat, as your retainers praise your deeds and find those who might  pay to meet you.
name: Legendary Leader

    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs=""
>
[Myth Hunter](/Feats.aspx?ID=8006) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=615" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=695" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #216: The Acropolis Pyre](/Sources.aspx?ID=271) pg. 82

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Hero-God Lore ](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) or Trained in [Legendary Beast Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8)

**Trigger**
You successfully [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) to identify a creature that has the [mythic](/Traits.aspx?ID=835) trait.

</column>

---

Your ancestors confronted and bested mythic beasts of Iblydan antiquity. Through a combination of hearing these tales and channeling a hint of those warriors' mythic power, you've developed a knack for bypassing a mythic creature's fabled defenses. The next time you successfully Strike the triggering creature, your Strike treats its mythic resistance as 2 lower. You treat its resistance as 4 lower if your Hero-God Lore or Legendary Beast Lore proficiency rank is expert, 6 lower if your rank is master, and 8 lower if your rank is legendary.

 If your Strike would already ignore mythic resistance (because you are a mythic character or wield a mythic weapon), your next successful Strike against the creature instead deals an additional 1d6 precision damage. This additional damage increases to 1d8 if your Hero-God Lore or Legendary Beast Lore proficiency rank is expert, 1d10 if your rank is master, or 1d12 if your rank is legendary.
name: Myth Hunter
prerequisite_markdown: Trained in [Hero-God Lore ](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) or Trained in [Legendary Beast Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8)

When you escape death, you return to life with retributive fire that burns those who harmed you. You expend 1 Mythic Point, return to consciousness, [Stand](/Actions.aspx?ID=2303), and regain additional Hit Points equal to your level. You then erupt in flames, dealing fire damage equal to twice your level to adjacent foes, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your class DC. Foes that critically fail the save are also [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 5 feet away from you.
name: Fiery Rebirth

    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Follow the Threads of Fate](/Feats.aspx?ID=8008) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Ikon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=840" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea
](/Sources.aspx?ID=273) pg. 79

</column>

---

A [norn’s](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=448) thread—spun from fate itself—runs through or wraps around your ikon. 

**Transcendence—Unravel the Future** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842)) You pluck a single thread from your ikon, which ties itself in three loops about your wrist, where it remains for the next minute. If you fail an attack roll or skill check, and a +1 status bonus would change your failure into a success or your success into a critical success, you gain a +1 status bonus to this check retroactively, changing the outcome appropriately as the thread pulls you to the future you desire. The thread then falls from your wrist. You can have only one thread in existence at a time.
name: Follow the Threads of Fate

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Topple the Titans](/Feats.aspx?ID=8009) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea
](/Sources.aspx?ID=273) pg. 79

**Frequency**
Once per minute

**Requirements**
Your previous action was a successful or critically successful [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) against a creature at least one size larger than you, and the enemy became [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

</column>

---

Even the greatest titans of the world are easier to kill when they’re lying on the ground. You’ve made it your mission to topple such giants and can make others fall with a force that shakes the very earth. The triggering creature’s toppling body shakes the ground, emitting a quake in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) from their space. Apply the result of your triggering [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the Reflex DC of each creature in the emanation to Trip them as well. You do not need to have a hand free, and you do not lose your balance if any of your attempts to Trip are a critical failure.
name: Topple the Titans
requirement_markdown: Your previous action was a successful or critically successful [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) against a creature at least one size larger than you, and the enemy became [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

You can voice these mysteries, forcing others to overcome them as you did. **Transcendence—Tangle in Riddle** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842)) You speak a bizarre koan that transcends language and is understood by only one creature within 30 feet. **Failure** The target is stupefied 1 for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Failure** The target is stupefied 1 and [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1d4 rounds.
name: Riddle the Sphinx

You stole a [gorgon’s](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=496) gaze and trapped it in your ikon. You’ve learned how to brandish it for your advantage, subduing the rage of the trapped gaze by turning it on your enemies. 

**Transcendence—Brandish the Gorgon’s Gaze** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Effect** You Raise your mirrored aegis, though it seems to lose some of its usual luster in favor of the dull sheen of stone. If you [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) a melee attack with the mirrored aegis before the beginning of your next turn, the gorgon’s eyes upon it flash at the triggering creature, who must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC. On a failure, they are [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 minute as their body partially petrifies. Regardless of the outcome, that creature is temporarily immune to this effect for 24 hours.
name: Shield of Stone

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Exemplar" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=839" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea
](/Sources.aspx?ID=273) pg. 79

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Prerequisites**
[plunderer of the hive’s riches](/Epithets.aspx?ID=17)

</column>

---

Though you may have stolen from the hive, you’ve nothing but respect for the armies guarding it, and you borrow their strength to overwhelm your foes. Your body transforms into a swarm of bees. You [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) up to twice your Speed; during this movement you can pass through any space that would be small enough for a single insect, and your movement does not trigger reactions. If you pass through an enemy’s space, you can inflict a storm of stings that deals 3d4 piercing damage and 3d4 poison damage, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save against your class DC. At the end of your movement, you return to your original form. At 10th level and every two levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d4 piercing damage and 1d4 poison damage.
name: As a Thousand Soldiers

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs=""
>
[Heroic Weakness](/Feats.aspx?ID=8014) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 65

**Archetype** [Godling](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=293)

**Prerequisites**
[Godling Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7392); [hero-god accomplishment](/Rules.aspx?ID=3489)

</column>

---

As word of your faith spreads, so too does word of your foibles and weaknesses. Where originally these disadvantages may have been myths themselves, or simply manifestations of ill fortune or chance, when you take this feat, one of those weaknesses becomes real. 

Work with your GM to choose your heroic weakness—it must be to a specific damage type and must be associated with a specific condition, both of which must be elements that are going to be significant parts of your story’s future. For example, you could choose for a [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to be [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage caused to you by a follower of a rival god, physical damage from a specific category of weapon, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage caused by a specific kind of [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage taken in a certain part of the world or from creatures from that part of the world. The amount of weakness you gain to this damage is equal to twice your level. 

But with this new weakness comes a new source of power, for when your foes would take advantage of your weakness, you can use the pain and humiliation to bolster your own resolve. You gain the following reaction. 

**Makes Me Stronger** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** You take damage from your heroic weakness and are not reduced to fewer than 1 Hit Point by the damage; **Effect** You make a show of powering through your weakness, be it by flexing your muscles, issuing a challenging roar, laughing off the pain, or simply acting as if the damage doesn’t worry you in the slightest. Your spells gain a status bonus to damage against the triggering creature equal to twice their rank; this lasts until the next time you use this reaction. In addition, the creature that damaged you with your heroic weakness must attempt a Will save against your class or spellcasting DC, whichever is higher, to avoid becoming [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure).
name: Heroic Weakness

[Myth of Quick-Thinking](/Feats.aspx?ID=8015) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 65

**Archetype** [Godling](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=293)

**Prerequisites**
[Godling Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7392); [hero-god accomplishment](/Rules.aspx?ID=3489)

</column>

---

Your myth includes amusing parables of clever plans and sly maneuvers, often applied to small things in life such as disguising yourself as a sheep to ambush a [wolf](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=485) or tricking a [snake](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=473) into shedding its skin. You can [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) as a two-action activity, regardless of how elaborate your tale. When you attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) to do so at [mythic proficiency](/Rules.aspx?ID=3322). If you succeed at a Deception check while in the presence of your hierophant, any of your worshippers that you can see automatically succeed at an attempt to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) or Lie in such a way that supports your story (for example, the target of your Lie claiming you are a teacher sees all of your worshippers as students). 

You also lay claim to one of the following domains of your choice: [confidence](/Domains.aspx?ID=67), [secrecy](/Domains.aspx?ID=91), or [trickery](/Domains.aspx?ID=94).
name: Myth of Quick-Thinking

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

**Prerequisites**
[Godling Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7392); [hero-god accomplishment](/Rules.aspx?ID=3489)

</column>

---

Your myth speaks of distant travel to countless countries, planes, or dimensions. When any effect attempts to anchor you to a plane (such as [_planar tether_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1636)) or to banish you from the current plane (such as [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448)), that effect gains the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait. If it already has the incapacitation trait, you gain a +4 status bonus to your save against the effect. If you successfully save against such an effect, the creature that attempted to use this effect on you must succeed at a Will save against your class or spell DC (whichever is higher) to avoid becoming [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 minute (stupefied 2 on a critical failure).

You also lay claim to one of the following domains of your choice: [dreams](/Domains.aspx?ID=72), [fate](/Domains.aspx?ID=75), or [travel](/Domains.aspx?ID=93).
name: Myth of Realm-Walking

    right="Feat 6"
    pfs=""
>
[Hero-God's Bond](/Feats.aspx?ID=8017) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 67

**Archetype** [Warrior Of Legend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=286)

**Prerequisites**
[Warrior Of Legend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7261)

</column>

---

At times, the myth of a hero-god includes a favored partner or a bonded ally, such as the hero-gods of promises and shield-bearers, Upion and Warrick. When you gain this feat, select an ally. They are your bonded partner. While you and your bonded partner are on the same plane, you and your bonded partner always have the same [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) value (defaulting to the highest value), so when your bonded partner’s doomed condition would increase or be set to a specific amount, your doomed value also increases to match (if it is lower than your current doomed value). When either of you are dying, the other knows this immediately, as well as the distance and direction to the dying partner’s location. When either of you attempts a [recovery check](/Rules.aspx?ID=2326), as long as the other is on the same plane, reduce the DC of the recovery check by 2. You can communicate telepathically with your bonded partner as long as you are within 60 feet of each other. 

You can change your bonded partner by spending 1 week of downtime bonding with another ally.

<title level="2">Hero-God's Bond leads to...</title>
[Grief's Fury](/Feats.aspx?ID=8019), [Martyr's Parry](/Feats.aspx?ID=8018)
name: Hero-God's Bond

A lost love. A broken [spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14). Rage burning through a hero-god. When a myth speaks of a wounded partner, the hero-god often rejects the future. Too many find themselves on a battlefield, clutching a body that they swore to protect. In that moment, the wellspring of power ignites. 

Increase the value of your [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) condition by 1, to a maximum of doomed 3. You gain a +2 status bonus to attack rolls against any creature that attacked or dealt damage to your partner in the last minute. If you reduce a creature to 0 HP that attacked or damaged your partner, you gain [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to twice their level. These benefits last until your bonded partner is no longer [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) or for 1 minute, whichever duration is shorter.
name: Grief's Fury

    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs=""
>
[Hydra's Bond](/Feats.aspx?ID=8020) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 68

</column>

---

When the hero-gods built Pol-Sylirica and its seven armies, they feared betrayal from within. They took inspiration from the multi-headed hydra, and bound the seven armies together with supernatural ties to make them behave as if they shared one body. Following these tactics, during [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), you can select a number of willing creatures equal to half your level to be part of your hydra’s bond. Members of the same hydra’s bond always know if another member is telling the truth. 

When a member of the hydra’s bond saves against an effect that would have them act against their allies (such as the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) condition, a [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501) spell, or other [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects that would give an ally the [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) condition), you can spend a Mythic Point to allow that character to attempt the save at mythic proficiency. If a member of the hydra’s bond fails a save against such an effect, other members automatically know that the target is controlled or under the influence of a mental effect. In addition, all members of the hydra’s bond gain a +2 circumstance bonus to any roll made to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) that effect.
name: Hydra's Bond
