    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs=""
>
[Fool's Fleeting Plan](/Feats.aspx?ID=8021) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 68

</column>

---

When Aercelys, hero-god of fools and fire, dove into the funeral pyre of his beloved daughter, he seemed a fool without thought for his safety. Yet when he returned with her body, he showed the slow rise of her chest, and those who doubted became the fools. 

You carry Aercelys’s spark within you, which protects you from rash decisions. You do not trigger reactions when you use an action that you gained from being [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89).
name: Fool's Fleeting Plan

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker **Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane ](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 68 --- At one time, the hero-gods could not tell their own prophecies. Ongalte, hero-god of deicide, defied this rule and cemented why it should be upheld. Those that can reach into the web of fates and pluck out a string are forbidden from speaking of their own myth. Yet nothing forbids them from speaking the myths of others. You peer through the world around you to see the delicate threads connecting others to their fate when they become afflicted. Choose a creature within 30 feet that you can see and who you know is affected by an ongoing affliction that progresses through stages, such as most [diseases](/Rules.aspx?ID=2767) or [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=669). Spend 1 [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323), then attempt a [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) check at [mythic proficiency](/Rules.aspx?ID=3322) against that affliction’s DC. **Critical Success** The next time the afflicted creature attempts a saving throw against the affliction, they automatically gain a critical success. If this results in the affliction coming to an end, you gain 1 Mythic Point.
**Success** The next time the afflicted creature attempts a saving throw against the affliction, they gain a +1 circumstance bonus to the roll and increase the result of the saving throw by one degree of success.
**Failure** The next time the afflicted creature attempts a saving throw against the affliction, they gain a +1 circumstance bonus.
**Critical Failure** You share the afflicted’s fate, and are exposed to their affliction. You must immediately save against the affliction’s effect.
name: Stages of Fate

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs=""
>
[Windborne Shove](/Feats.aspx?ID=8023) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 69

**Prerequisites**
[guardian's calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=6)

</column>

---

The hero-god of phalanxes and winds, grand Konebrys, led a force of dozens against an army of thousands. They gathered their soldiers in tight ranks, then marched against the wind to take the enemy by surprise. When a gust pushed the foes back, they struck their victorious blow. 

You emulate Konebrys’s tactics in pushing small forces against mighty foes. Whenever you are adjacent to an ally, creatures that attempt to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370) your space take a –2 circumstance penalty to their [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check.

When you critically succeed on a melee Strike against an enemy, you can immediately attempt to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) your target as a free action. You can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) on this Shove attempt to do so at [mythic proficiency](/Rules.aspx?ID=3322).
name: Windborne Shove

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers **Source** [Gatewalkers Player's Guide (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=287) pg. 11 --- Your touch opens a conduit into the mind of your target, the emptiness within you pulling skills and memories through. You touch an adjacent creature and choose any skill you believe they are trained or better in. If you choose a skill that they are trained or better in, you borrow their proficiency in that skill, becoming trained in that skill for 1 minute. If they are not trained in the skill you choose, the GM chooses the next most applicable skill (for instance, choosing [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) instead of [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36)). At 12th level, you can become an expert in the skill if the target is as well. If the target of Borrowed Ability is unwilling, the effects depend on the target’s Will save. **Critical Success** The target resists your touch completely.
**Success** Your borrowing is unstable. At the beginning of each of your turns, you must either relinquish your hold on the borrowed skill (as a free action), or attempt another backlash check for your deviation; the ability ends if you fail.
**Failure** Borrowed Ability lasts for its full duration.
name: Borrowed Ability

All creatures in the cone must attempt a Fortitude saving throw. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is pulled 10 feet toward you.

Creatures that would be pulled into your square contact the void, which deals 5d6 spirit damage with no saving throw, and are then spat back out in a square adjacent to you of your choice. At 12th level, and every 2 levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d6.

An invisible arm extends from you to grab and manipulate objects. The arm reaches up to 15 feet, grabs an unattended object of 1 Bulk or less, and immediately deposits it in one of your free hands or at your feet. Alternatively, it reaches up to 15 feet and performs a simple [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action like pushing open a window, though it can’t perform actions that require significant mechanical dexterity. For every 5 levels you have, the amount the hand can lift increases by 1 Bulk, and the arm’s range increases by 15 feet. Being ghostly, the arm can affect [ghosts](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=418), spirits, and other [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) entities, though in most cases, you need an awakening for this to be useful.
name: Ghostly Grasp (Deviant)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs=""
>
[Distant Wandering](/Feats.aspx?ID=8045) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Deviant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=426" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers Player's Guide (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=287) pg. 13, [Dark Archives (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=293) pg. 103

</column>

---

Your body falls unconscious and your spirit projects out of it. While in spirit form, you can’t consciously move your body, and you are [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) and inaudible, though a creature still might notice the signs of your presence and passing, just like for an invisible creature affected by [_silence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1674), or a [_scouting eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661). You can freely move about, though you can’t touch or move anything, cast spells, attack, or otherwise affect anything around you. Despite your ghostly form, you are not [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) and can’t pass through barriers you couldn’t in your body. Most effects can’t harm your spirit form, though some spells, like [_spirit blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685) and [_spirit song_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2029), explicitly damage a creature’s spirit.

You can return to your body as a free action. If you are in spirit form at the beginning of your turn, you must attempt another backlash check for your deviation, returning to your body if you fail.

**Awakening** Your spirit moves at the speed of thought. You gain a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) and a +20-foot status bonus to your Speed while in spirit form.

**Awakening** Time seems to pass more slowly while you are projecting your spirit. When you enter spirit form, you can [Recall Knowledge](/Rules.aspx?ID=2638) or [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301). You are [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) while in spirit form and can use the extra action only to Recall Knowledge or Seek.
name: Distant Wandering

markdown:
**Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 211 --- You have developed your magical breath. You expel the dragonet breath associated with your heritage. Dragonet Breath gains the traits listed in parentheses, and the DC is your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. After you use Dragonet Breath, you can’t use it again for 1d4 rounds.
  • **Fey Dragonet** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) You breathe a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of euphoric gas. Each creature in the area must succeed at a Fortitude save or become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round.
  • **Homing Drake** ([acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524)) You expel a 30-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) of caustic spittle that deals 1d4 acid damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. At 3rd level and every 2 levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d4.
  • **House Drake** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) You breathe out a cloud of silver mist in a 15-foot cone that deals 1d4 mental damage with a basic Will save. It counts as silver for the purpose of weaknesses. At 3rd level and every 2 levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d4.
  • **Pearl Dragonet** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566)) You breathe a 15-foot cone of golden mist that alters the luck of creatures caught in it. Each time you use Dragonet Breath, decide if it gives good luck or bad luck. Any creature affected by your Dragonet Breath becomes temporarily immune to it for 1 hour. If your breath gives good luck, each creature in the area gains a +1 status bonus to its next attack roll or skill check before the start of your next turn. If your breath gives bad luck, each creature in the area must attempt a Will save. A creature that fails its save takes a –1 status penalty to its next attack roll or skill check before the start of your next turn. On a critical failure, the creature must instead roll twice and take the worse result on the check; this is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect.
  • **Tidepool Dragonet** (fire) You breathe a 15-foot line of superheated gas that deals 1d4 fire damage with a basic Reflex save. At 3rd level and every 2 levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d4. You can use your breath underwater even though it has the fire trait. If you do, it gains the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) trait and emerges as a 20-foot cone of boiling water.
Dragonet Breath leads to... [Inhale, Exhale!](/Feats.aspx?ID=8054)
name: Dragonet Breath

</traits>

Your claws are as sharp as your teeth. You gain a [claw](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356) unarmed attack that deals 1d6 slashing damage. Your claws are in the [knife](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=9) group and have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) traits. 

At 5th level, whenever you get a critical hit with your claws or jaws, you get its [critical specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=2203) effect.

**Special** If your dragonet heritage is house drake, your claws count as [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920).

---

Your scales are hard as armor, ensuring you always have some protection in the dangerous world. When you’re unarmored, your scales give you a +1 item bonus to AC with a Dexterity cap of +3. The item bonus increases to +2 at 5th level. The item bonus to AC from these scales is cumulative with [_armor potency_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2785) runes on your [explorer’s clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=39), the [_mystic armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1611) spell, or [_bands of force_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3058).
name: Scales of Steel

You are protective of your hoard, and woe betide anyone who tries to take what’s yours. Make a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against the triggering creature. This Strike doesn’t count toward your multiple attack penalty, and your multiple attack penalty doesn’t apply to this Strike.

</traits>

](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 212

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Without a limit to usage, this feat grants infinite focus points._

</column>

---

You form a close bond with a companion, similar to that of a master and familiar, that lets you gift them with a touch of your inherent magic. You can spend time with another player character during your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575) to make them your designated companion for that day. That player must have a [Focus](/Rules.aspx?ID=285) Point pool. You gain the Fortify Focus activity. 

**Fortify Focus**<actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Requirements** You are touching your designated companion, and their focus pool isn’t full; **Effect** Your companion regains 1 Focus Point.
name: Bond Companion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Form a Flock](/Feats.aspx?ID=8060) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Dragonet" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=893" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 213

**Frequency**
once per day

</column>

---

You call nearby wild dragonets to swarm to your side. The flock grants you 25 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last up to 1 minute. The flock disperses and the effect ends when the temporary Hit Points are gone, the minute elapses, or you [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) Form a Flock. As long as you’re surrounded by your flock, you gain [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 5 to area and splash damage and have a flock aura in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Enemies in this aura are [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65). 

When you use Form a Flock and while it lasts, you can take the Acrid Barrage action.

**Acrid Barrage** <actions string="Two Actions" /> **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Each enemy in your aura takes 5d6 acid damage and 2d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. The initial damage increases to 6d6 at 15th level and 7d6 at 18th level.
name: Form a Flock

Through your wiles, charm, or a little bit of magic, you lure in a foe for a closer look—and a quick bite! Choose a creature within 30 feet. It must attempt a Will save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. If the target fails its save, you pull it up to 30 feet. If the target ends this movement in reach of your jaws, you can attempt a jaws Strike against it. Regardless of the result of its save, the target is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
name: Lurching Chomp

Your scales have taken on the characteristics of your draconic benefactor and insulate you from certain damage types. You gain resistance equal to half your level to the damage type dealt by the breath of your draconic benefactor. Examples are shown on the Draconic Benefactors table [here.](DraconicBenefactors.aspx) 

If your draconic benefactor’s breath deals bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage, instead of gaining resistance to that type, you must choose [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697).

You possess features that dragons find particularly cute or charming, making it easier to endear yourself to them, or at least cause them to momentarily forgive your insolence. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) checks and saving throws against dragons. In addition, whenever you meet a creature with the [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=581) trait in a social situation, you can attempt a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on that creature immediately, rather than after conversing for 1 minute; you take a –5 circumstance penalty to this check. If you fail, you can choose to engage in 1 minute of conversation and then attempt a new check rather than accept the failure or critical failure result.
name: Draconic Sycophant

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragonscaled Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=8066) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Kobold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=224" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 214

**Prerequisites**
[Dragonscaled Kobold](/Heritages.aspx?ID=334) heritage

</column>

---

The study of dragons is not merely academic to you but part of your family tree. Tracking lineages, benefactors, and communities has become second nature. You become trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) and your choice of [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46). For each of these skills in which you were already trained, you become trained in a skill of your choice. You also gain the [Additional Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=5114) general feat for [Dragon Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41).
name: Dragonscaled Lore

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hoarder's Craw](/Feats.aspx?ID=8067) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Kobold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=224" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 214

**Prerequisites**
[Dragonscaled Kobold](/Heritages.aspx?ID=334) heritage

</column>

---

Your draconic features include a pouch inside your body that you use to store food prior to digestion. However, instead of swallowing rocks or other minerals to help grind your food, you swallow gold and other precious ores for the same purpose. Inside your craw, you can store up to 100 coins (of any type) or an amount of precious material that’s light Bulk (usually a chunk). Items can be concealed inside your craw or regurgitated as an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action. Instead of precious material or coins, you can also use your craw to conceal an item of light Bulk but can only do so for up to 1 hour before you must regurgitate it. While carrying anything other than precious material or coins in your craw, you become [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 due to the discomfort.
name: Hoarder's Craw

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kobold Breath](/Feats.aspx?ID=8068) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Kobold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=224" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 215

**Prerequisites**
[Dragonscaled Kobold](/Heritages.aspx?ID=334) heritage

</column>

---

You channel the power of your [draconic benefactor](DraconicBenefactors.aspx) to expel a torrent of energy from your mouth that manifests as a 30-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) or a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386), dealing 1d4 damage. Each creature in the area must attempt a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) saving throw against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. You can’t use this ability again for 1d4 rounds.

At 3rd level and every 2 levels after that, the damage increases by 1d4. The breath shape, damage type, and the saving throw match those of your draconic benefactor, such as those shown on the Draconic Benefactors table here. This activity has the traits associated with the magical tradition of your draconic benefactor and the type of damage the breath deals.

<title level="2">Kobold Breath leads to...</title>
[Breath Unleashed](/Feats.aspx?ID=8072), [Draconic Paragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=8074), [Kobold Momentum](/Feats.aspx?ID=8070)
name: Kobold Breath

</column>

---

Your [benefactor’s](DraconicBenefactors.aspx)  blood flows through your veins, and you channel their power into your body. When you critically hit with an unarmed Strike, the target takes an additional 1 [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) of the same type as the damage dealt by the breath of your draconic benefactor. Some examples are shown on the Draconic Benefactors table [here.](DraconicBenefactors.aspx)

<title level="2">Benefactor's Strike leads to...</title>
[Draconic Paragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=8074)

    right="Feat 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kobold Momentum](/Feats.aspx?ID=8070) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Kobold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=224" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 215

**Prerequisites**
[Kobold Breath](/Feats.aspx?ID=8068)

</column>

---

You possess the confidence of a dragon, and your success emboldens you to draw on power reserves you didn’t know you had. You recharge your Kobold Breath whenever you score a critical hit with a Strike. In addition, if you score a critical hit with a Strike and then use your Kobold Breath on the same turn, you can reroll any 1s on the damage dice. You must use the new result, even if it’s a 1.
name: Kobold Momentum

    pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 215

</column>

---

You’re a cunning adversary in close quarters and are well-versed in hit-and-run tactics that exploit the advantages of your small stature. As part of your training, you learned what to do when you’re [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90). You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to checks to Escape, and after resolving a successful [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) action, you can immediately use your reaction to [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) or to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) the creature from whom you just Escaped.
name: Tip the Scales

[Kobold Breath](/Feats.aspx?ID=8068)

</column>

---

You’re able to channel more of your draconic benefactor’s power and focus it into your Kobold Breath, increasing its effectiveness but requiring more time to recover. When you use Kobold Breath, you can increase the damage dice to d8s and increase the area to 30 feet for a [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) or 60 feet for a [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388). If you do, you can’t use Kobold Breath again for 1 hour. During this time, Kobold Breath cannot be recharged using [Kobold Momentum](/Feats.aspx?ID=8070).
name: Breath Unleashed

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dracomancer](/Feats.aspx?ID=8073) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Kobold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=224" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 215

**Prerequisites**
[Dragonscaled Kobold](/Heritages.aspx?ID=334) heritage

</column>

---

The power infused into you by your draconic benefactor’s presence allows you to manifest their most iconic spells. Choose one 1st-rank spell and one 2nd-rank spell from those listed for a dragon spellcaster of your draconic benefactor’s type, such as those presented here (for example, [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) and [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) for a [conspirator dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2936) benefactor). You can cast each of these spells once per day as [innate](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) spells of the same tradition as your draconic benefactor. You gain the trained proficiency rank in the spell attack modifier and spell DC statistics, and your key spellcasting attribute is Charisma.

<title level="2">Dracomancer leads to...</title>
[Draconic Paragon](/Feats.aspx?ID=8074), [Elite Dracomancer](/Feats.aspx?ID=8075)
name: Dracomancer

You gain additional effects for the prerequisite kobold feat. If you have more than one of the prerequisite feats, you gain the appropriate additional effects.
  • **Benefactor’s Strike** Your unarmed strikes made with your [jaws, claws, or tail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356) gain the [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=570) trait.
  • **Dracomancer** Increase the number of times per day you can cast each of the granted 1st and 2nd-rank [innate spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) by 1.
  • **Kobold Breath** Creatures that critically fail their save against your Kobold Breath take 3d4 [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) of the type dealt by your draconic benefactor.
name: Draconic Paragon

](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 216

---

Your blood surges with the might of dragons who master the waters, and you can move freely in their domain. You gain a [swim Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2351) of 15 feet; if you already have a base swim Speed, it increases by 5 feet. You can hold your breath underwater for 10 minutes before needing air, and you take only a –1 circumstance melee penalty to slashing or bludgeoning attacks that pass through water (instead of –2). If you choose a draconic exemplar, you must choose a dragon with a swim Speed.

<title level="2">Aqueous Dragonblood leads to...</title>
[Home in the Deep](/Feats.aspx?ID=8084)
name: Aqueous Dragonblood

You also gain the [Combat Climber](/Feats.aspx?ID=5134) skill feat, even if you aren’t trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36). If you choose a draconic exemplar, you must choose a dragon with a climb Speed.

Your connection to the divine has sanctified your blood, but the power only manifests when you are shedding it. Choose [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521). While you are taking [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), you can [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to coat a piercing or slashing weapon you’re wielding with your blood. Until the end of your turn, your Strikes with that weapon deal an additional 1d6 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage with the chosen trait to creatures with the opposing trait. Alternatively, you can deal 1d6 slashing damage to yourself (which can’t be resisted in any way) as part of that Interact action if you aren’t taking persistent bleed damage.
name: Blood and Spirit

This bonus also applies to saving throws against spells or effects that attempt to force you to move.
